title: [apiv2] only outputs container ID whatever running any command inside a running container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080

2. ./bin/podman run --name myalpine -td docker.io/library/alpine:latest /bin/sh

3. curl -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"AttachStderr": true,"AttachStdin": true,"AttachStdout": true,"Cmd":["ps"]}' http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/my
alpine/exec

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -s -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"AttachStderr": true,"AttachStdin": true,"AttachStdout": true,"Cmd":["ps"]}' http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/contain
ers/myalpine/exec
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/myalpine/exec HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/json
> Content-Length: 76
>
* upload completely sent off: 76 out of 76 bytes
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 201 Created
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Fri, 15 May 2020 10:08:22 GMT
< Content-Length: 74
<
{"Id":"dab67b13ed811abffdc46f2c9440627c58752996bb5213db55d1c9a14c2e48ca"}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -s -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"AttachStderr": true,"AttachStdin": true,"AttachStdout": true,"Cmd":["ls"]}' http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/contain
ers/myalpine/exec
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/myalpine/exec HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/json
> Content-Length: 76
>
* upload completely sent off: 76 out of 76 bytes
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 201 Created
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Fri, 15 May 2020 10:12:10 GMT
< Content-Length: 74
<
{"Id":"4161676c34490cb01a9856726fc406a6604c38665e6f3781694019a653d3af7a"}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
can output correct command result 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         4611ff5ce4ff67a142363ab66e10944bfc40f860
Built:              Fri May 15 03:26:27 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body: Exec is only partially implemented right now; #5831 has most of the rest.

This should be working since #5831 is landed, so I'm going to close.

Resize is still pending on another PR from @jwhonce though.
Feel free to reopen if exec issues persist.

> 
> 
> This should be working since #5831 is landed, so I'm going to close.
> 
> Resize is still pending on another PR from @jwhonce though.

Got the same result like before and w/o command output. 

```shell
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ git rev-parse HEAD
1a93857acc4ee1e5a9213e2c22f12802d84cd277

$ curl -v -s -H 'Content-type: application/json' -d '{"AttachStderr": true,"AttachStdin": true,"AttachStdout": true,"Cmd":["ls"]}' http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/myctr/exec
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/myctr/exec HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-type: application/json
> Content-Length: 76
>
* upload completely sent off: 76 out of 76 bytes
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 201 Created
< Content-Type: application/json
< Date: Tue, 07 Jul 2020 09:00:21 GMT
< Content-Length: 74
<
{"Id":"045a7e4a2b7457f6dd242c996db67b26efb65182189f6079f3129679a0200cac"}
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
```

Same when using the remote client:

> $ /bin/podman-remote --url tcp://127.0.0.1:8080 exec d8 ls

... no output.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@chuanchang , you must create an exec session first
```
$ id=`curl -s \
  -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
  -d '{"AttachStderr": true,"AttachStdin": true,"AttachStdout": true,"Cmd":["ls"]}' \
  http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/containers/myctr/exec | jq -r .Id `
```
and start it
```
$ curl -o - -X POST http://localhost:8081/v1.40/libpod/exec/${id}/start -d '{"Detach":false,"Tty":false}' -H 'Content-type: application/json'
sbin
dev
docker-entrypoint.d
docker-entrypoint.sh
etc
home
lib
media
mnt
opt
proc
root
run
sbin
srv
sys
tmp
usr
var
[conmon:d]: exec with attach is waiting for start message from parent
[conmon:d]: exec with attach got start message from parent
```
and the version info
```
$ podman --version
podman version 2.0.4
```
